purpose: Implements the Notepad++ dockable preview pane. Registers the 'MarkdownPlusPlusNativePreview' window class, creates the WS_CHILD HWND parented to Notepad++, hosts a WebViewHost in its client area, registers itself as a dockable dialog via NPPM_DMMREGASDCKDLG (DWS_DF_CONT_RIGHT default), and forwards Show/Hide via NPPM_DMMSHOW/NPPM_DMMHIDE. Watches WM_SIZE for resize, WM_ACTIVATE to force a WebView Reload when the pane regains focus (this is the 'blank content on window context switch' fix), WM_NOTIFY/DMN_CLOSE to fire the close callback, and WM_DESTROY for cleanup.
calls_out_to: User32: RegisterClassExW / CreateWindowExW / DestroyWindow / DefWindowProcW / SetWindowLongPtrW / GetWindowLongPtrW / SendMessageW / LoadCursorW
WinUtil CombinePath / GetModuleDirectory (for the assets root passed to WebViewHost)
invariants: Create may be called only once per PreviewWindow instance - a second call short-circuits if hwnd_ is already set.
Both Destroy() and WM_DESTROY tear down the WebViewHost before clearing hwnd_; the dtor also calls Destroy so RAII is safe.
WindowProc routes via GWLP_USERDATA: WM_NCCREATE captures the `this` pointer from CREATESTRUCTW.lpCreateParams. Messages before WM_NCCREATE fall through to DefWindowProcW.
The pane is WS_CHILD and parented to the Notepad++ HWND; Notepad++ docking manager re-parents it as the user docks/floats.
gotchas: DMN_CLOSE is the literal value 1051 - it is hard-coded here because it is not in NppApi.h. Keep them in sync if NppApi.h ever picks it up.
WM_ACTIVATE -> webView_.Reload() exists to work around a WebView2 bug where the page goes blank after a window-context switch (commit a2aefaa); removing it brings the regression back.

RegisterDockableWindow stores raw const wchar_t* pointers (pszName/pszModuleName) in NppTbData - the strings here are static literals so lifetime is fine; any future dynamic strings would need to outlive Notepad++'s read.
